title: Ensure noexec option set on /tmp partition

prose.en: Ensure noexec option set on /tmp partition

description.en: The `noexec` mount option specifies that the filesystem cannot contain executable binaries.

rationale.en: Since the `/tmp` filesystem is only intended for temporary file storage, set this option to ensure that users cannot run executable binaries from `/tmp`.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/tmp`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `noexec` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/tmp` partition.  

Example:  

Run the following command to remount `/tmp` with the configured options:  

Impact:  

Setting the `noexec` option on `/tmp` may prevent installation and/or updating of some 3rd party software.

```
# mount -o remount /tmp
```

```
 /tmp         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/tmp`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `noexec` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/tmp` partition.  

Example:  

Run the following command to remount `/tmp` with the configured options:  

Impact:  

Setting the `noexec` option on `/tmp` may prevent installation and/or updating of some 3rd party software.

```
# mount -o remount /tmp
```

```
 /tmp         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

references: See the fstab(5) manual page for more information.
